trim_reasons: Spread of -17.5 is the largest on the slate; with a min_floor of 22.0 against an avg of 29.0, a genuine blowout produces a 7-minute minutes gap that directly threatens counting stats. The blowout penalty was applied but the 7-minute floor-to-avg gap is structurally wide for a REB prop.

Castle is tagged VOLATILE with a min_floor of 22.9 against a -17.5 spread — the same three-factor fragility stack (VOLATILE + min_floor < 24 + spread_abs ≥ 13) that audit history recommends formalizing as a hard skip. A secondary scorer on a heavily favored team is the player most exposed to early bench rest in a true blowout, and the VOLATILE tag means his distribution is wide enough that a short-minutes game produces a clean miss.

Despite a 90% overall hit rate at T10, the -17.5 blowout spread already forced a step-down from T25/T20/T15 to T10, signaling the system's own concern about minutes compression; if SA builds a large lead early, Fox — not Wembanyama — is the more likely candidate for early rest as a recent trade acquisition whose team may manage his workload.

B2B + blowout risk (spread -14.5) is a dual-suppression stack: even though the B2B hit rate at T15 is strong at 92%, a genuine blowout in a game where ATL is a heavy favorite means a secondary scorer's minutes could be curtailed in Q4, and the 8-minute floor-to-avg gap (26.9 vs 33.2) leaves meaningful counting-stat exposure if rest decisions kick in.
